extra.reasoning: The property is monotonicity with respect to chest_pain: setting chest_pain=true while holding all other inputs fixed must never lower acuity. The decisive fact is that chest_pain appears in NO rule. The seven rules trigger only on fever (r1), slurred_speech (r2), dyspnea (r3), stiff_neck (r4), confusion (r5), vomiting (r6), and pallor (r7). The default sets routine when no rule fires. Since chest_pain is never read by any rule or the default, flipping it from false to true leaves the set of matching rules, their priorities, the tie-breaking, and the default fallback completely unchanged. Hence acuity is identical before and after setting chest_pain=true for every possible assignment of the other seven inputs. Equal acuity satisfies 'no lower than before' (equality is >= in the enum order). Therefore the property holds across the entire input space; no counterexample exists.
